title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Language Clinic: File Identity
summary: The command concatenates strings into paths, follows any symlink, accepts directories and devices, reads before checking size, and reports every failure as file not found. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the file identity increment in bounded file-admission policy. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.
Reject inputs beyond a declared byte budget before unbounded allocation and preserve actual versus allowed size evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-paths-file-admission-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_paths_file_admission_3(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Build File Identity evidence · Theory File Identity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-file-identity-95mhpm" that uses case 9109 to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-file-identity-95mhpm
# Competency: bookbot-file-identity.
# Case 9109: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_file_identity_95mhpm(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_95mhpm = "95mhpm-mphm59-9109"
    assert evidence_variant_95mhpm
    evidence_axes_95mhpm = (
        "corpus95mhpm",
        "policymphm59",
        "revision9109",
        "command95mhpm9109",
        "reportmphm599109",
        "test910995mhpm",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_95mhpm)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"

For Bookbot and Python Defend the File Identity model · Theory File Identity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A filename uniquely identifies unchanged content over time.
